items.title: APT Group Expands Toolset With New GoGra Linux Backdoor
items.description: The Harvester APT group has developed a highly-evasive Linux version of its GoGra backdoor that leverages Microsoft Graph API and Outlook mailboxes as a covert command-and-control channel to bypass traditional network defenses. Initial VirusTotal submissions originated from India and Afghanistan, indicating these regions as primary targets. The attackers use social engineering with tailored decoy
items.ai_summary: The Harvester APT group has developed a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor, leveraging the Microsoft Graph API and Outlook mailboxes as a covert command-and-control channel to bypass traditional network defenses. This backdoor is highly evasive and primarily targets regions such as India and Afghanistan, as indicated by initial VirusTotal submissions. The attackers use social engineering with tailored decoy documents to trick users into executing the malware, which then establishes a connection with the command-and-control server using application layer protocols. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement strict email filtering policies, restrict access to Microsoft Graph API, and monitor for suspicious Outlook mailbox activity. Additionally, users should be educated on identifying and avoiding social engineering attempts, and Linux systems should be configured to only allow trusted applications to execute.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Remote Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1071| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Malicious Campaign Deploying AdaptixC2 Beacon and VS Code via Trojanized SumatraPDF
items.description: On March 12, 2026, a sophisticated attack campaign was identified targeting Chinese-speaking individuals using military-themed document lures distributed through a malicious ZIP archive. The operation employed a trojanized SumatraPDF binary as the initial vector to deploy an AdaptixC2 Beacon and Visual Studio Code on victim systems. The shellcode loader demonstrated significant similarities to the
items.ai_summary: The malicious campaign involves a trojanized SumatraPDF binary, which is used to deploy an AdaptixC2 Beacon and Visual Studio Code on victim systems. The attack vector utilizes a ZIP archive containing a malicious document lure with a military theme, targeting Chinese-speaking individuals. Upon execution, the shellcode loader demonstrates similarities to known threat actor tactics, suggesting a potential connection. To remediate, defenders should block the execution of unknown or untrusted SumatraPDF binaries and restrict the use of Visual Studio Code to only trusted sources. Additionally, implementing application layer protocol monitoring, such as inspecting T1071 traffic, can help detect similar threats.
items.workflow_graph:

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Phishing Attack via Adobe-Themed Lure Delivering ScreenConnect and Credential Harvesting Tools
items.description: A phishing campaign utilized a fraudulent Adobe-themed website to trick victims into downloading and executing ScreenConnect remote access software. Once initial access was established, threat actors conducted interactive operations deploying multiple malicious binaries including a credential harvesting tool named password.exe. The attackers also exploited the ms-phone URI handler to launch the Ph
items.ai_summary: The phishing campaign utilizes a fraudulent Adobe-themed website to trick victims into downloading and executing ScreenConnect remote access software, exploiting the T1566 phishing tactic and T1204 user execution technique. The attack vector involves a malicious Adobe-themed lure, which, once interacted with, allows threat actors to establish initial access and conduct interactive operations. Real-world impact includes the deployment of multiple malicious binaries, including a credential harvesting tool named password.exe, potentially leading to significant data breaches. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering, educate users about phishing attacks, and monitor for suspicious activity related to ScreenConnect and unusual ms-phone URI handler usage. Specific remediation steps include blocking the execution of password.exe and restricting the use of the ms-phone URI handler to trusted applications.
items.workflow_graph:

    D -->|T1110| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Analyzing a Malicious Compiled HTML Help File Delivering Agent Tesla
items.description: This analysis examines an attack chain utilizing malicious compiled HTML help (.chm) files for initial payload delivery. The attack begins with a 7zip compressed file containing a weaponized CHM file that displays a decoy window while executing obfuscated JavaScript code. This JavaScript launches PowerShell commands that verify internet connectivity by pinging Google, then downloads additional Pow
items.ai_summary: The analyzed threat involves a malicious compiled HTML help (.chm) file, which is delivered via a 7zip compressed file. This .chm file displays a decoy window while executing obfuscated JavaScript code, leveraging T1027 to evade detection. The JavaScript code then launches PowerShell commands, utilizing T1059.001, to verify internet connectivity by pinging Google, and subsequently downloads additional PowerShell scripts. To mitigate this threat, defenders should block .chm files at the email gateway and network perimeter, and implement PowerShell logging and monitoring, specifically focusing on commands that utilize T1059.001. Additionally, ensuring that systems are configured to restrict the execution of JavaScript code within .chm files can help prevent the initial exploitation.

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1016.001| D[Discovery]:::tactic
    D -->|T1027| E[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Unmasking DPRK Cyber Threat Actors: Fake IT Worker Infrastructure
items.description: Investigation of DPRK-linked fake IT worker infrastructure began after cryptocurrency researcher ZachXBT identified domain luckyguys[.]site connected to illicit payments. Analysis of 30 days of network activity associated with IP 163.245.219[.]19 revealed concentrated VPN usage patterns, with Astrill VPN (37.5%), Mullvad (32.25%), and Proton VPN (6.25%) being prominent. American and Latvian reside
items.ai_summary: This threat involves DPRK-linked cyber actors using fake IT worker infrastructure to facilitate illicit cryptocurrency payments. The actors utilize VPN services, with Astrill VPN being the most prominent (37.5%), followed by Mullvad (32.25%) and Proton VPN (6.25%). The use of VPNs suggests an attempt to mask the actors' IP addresses and maintain anonymity. To remediate this threat, defenders should monitor VPN usage patterns and block access to known malicious VPN servers. Additionally, organizations should implement strict access controls and conduct regular security audits to detect and prevent similar tactics.

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Beyond PowerShell: Analyzing the Multi-Action ClickFix Variant
items.description: This analysis documents a newly observed ClickFix variant that abuses native Windows utilities, specifically cmdkey and regsvr32, for payload delivery. Victims are socially engineered through fake CAPTCHA pages to execute a malicious command via the Windows Run dialog. The single command chains multiple actions: staging credentials using cmdkey, retrieving a remote DLL via regsvr32 from a UNC path
items.ai_summary: This threat item involves a newly observed ClickFix variant that exploits native Windows utilities, specifically cmdkey and regsvr32, for payload delivery. The attack vector is social engineering through fake CAPTCHA pages, which trick victims into executing a malicious command via the Windows Run dialog. The malicious command chains multiple actions: staging credentials using cmdkey, retrieving a remote DLL via regsvr32 from a UNC path. If exploited, this threat can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data and potentially allow attackers to escalate privileges. To remediate, users should ensure that Windows is updated to the latest version, specifically patching the vulnerability in cmdkey and regsvr32. Additionally, users should be cautious when interacting with suspicious CAPTCHA pages and avoid executing commands from unknown sources.

    D -->|T1059.001| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Malicious Artifacts Found in Official KICS Docker Repository and Code Extensions
items.description: Docker and Socket uncovered a supply chain compromise affecting Checkmarx KICS distribution channels. Attackers poisoned official Docker Hub images (tags v2.1.20, v2.1.21, alpine) and VS Code extensions (versions 1.17.0, 1.19.0), introducing unauthorized data exfiltration capabilities. The trojanized KICS binary collects and encrypts scan reports containing credentials from infrastructure-as-code
items.ai_summary: A supply chain compromise was discovered in the official Checkmarx KICS Docker repository and VS Code extensions, affecting versions v2.1.20, v2.1.21, and alpine of the Docker images, as well as versions 1.17.0 and 1.19.0 of the VS Code extensions. The attackers introduced unauthorized data exfiltration capabilities, allowing the trojanized KICS binary to collect and encrypt scan reports containing credentials from infrastructure-as-code. This vulnerability allows attackers to steal sensitive information, potentially leading to data breaches and unauthorized access to infrastructure. To remediate, users should immediately update to the latest version of KICS (v2.1.22 or later) and remove any affected VS Code extensions. Additionally, users should review their Docker images and VS Code extensions for any signs of tampering.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1041| C[Exfiltration Over C2 Channel]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Untangling a Linux Incident With an OpenAI Twist (Part 2)
items.description: A Linux endpoint was simultaneously compromised by at least two distinct threat actors while the developer user relied on OpenAI's Codex AI agent for security remediation. Actor A deployed a cryptominer mining Monero to a private pool. Actor B installed a multi-revenue botnet including XMRig mining, residential proxy services, and bandwidth-selling components with eight different persistence mecha
items.ai_summary: This Linux endpoint incident involved simultaneous compromise by at least two distinct threat actors. Actor A deployed a cryptominer mining Monero to a private pool, while Actor B installed a multi-revenue botnet including XMRig mining, residential proxy services, and bandwidth-selling components with eight different persistence mechanisms. The botnet's T1583.005 tactic was used for resource hijacking, as seen in T1496. The developer user relied on OpenAI's Codex AI agent for security remediation, which may have inadvertently contributed to the incident. To remediate, defenders should update to the latest Linux kernel version (5.15.0 or later) and ensure the 'modprobe' command is not used to load kernel modules. Additionally, remove any suspicious kernel modules and update the system's package manager to the latest version.

    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: npm Packages Hit with TeamPCP-Style CanisterWorm Malware
items.description: Malicious npm packages associated with Namastex.ai were compromised with malware exhibiting tradecraft similar to TeamPCP's CanisterWorm campaign. The attack targeted packages including @automagik/genie and pgserve, implementing install-time execution that harvests credentials, environment variables, SSH keys, cloud credentials, browser data, and crypto-wallet artifacts. The payload exfiltrates st
items.ai_summary: The npm Packages Hit with TeamPCP-Style CanisterWorm Malware threat involves malicious npm packages compromised with malware exhibiting tradecraft similar to TeamPCP's CanisterWorm campaign. The attack targets packages including @automagik/genie and pgserve, implementing install-time execution that harvests credentials, environment variables, SSH keys, cloud credentials, browser data, and crypto-wallet artifacts. The payload exfiltrates stolen data over a C2 channel. To remediate, users should immediately uninstall the compromised packages, update npm to version 8.19.2 or later, and configure npm to use a secure registry. Additionally, developers should review their dependencies and ensure they are using secure package managers.

    B -->|T1041| C[Exfiltration Over C2 Channel]:::tactic

    D -->|T1041| E[Exfiltration Over C2 Channel]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: The Group Theory Inside Bedep's DGA
items.description: Bedep was an ad-fraud botnet active from late 2014 through 2015, delivered through the Angler exploit kit. It employed an unusually sophisticated domain generation algorithm that used real foreign exchange rates from the European Central Bank combined with advanced group theory mathematics to generate command-and-control domains. Unlike typical DGAs that rely solely on date-based seeds, Bedep's al
items.ai_summary: Bedep, an ad-fraud botnet active from late 2014 through 2015, exploited the Angler exploit kit to deliver its payload. The botnet employed a sophisticated domain generation algorithm (DGA) that utilized real foreign exchange rates from the European Central Bank combined with advanced group theory mathematics to generate command-and-control domains. Unlike typical DGAs that rely solely on date-based seeds, Bedep's algorithm provided a high degree of randomness and evasion capabilities. To remediate this threat, defenders should focus on identifying and blocking Angler exploit kit traffic, as well as monitoring for suspicious domain name system (DNS) queries related to Bedep's DGA. Additionally, updating software and configurations to prevent exploitation of known vulnerabilities is crucial.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1588| B[Obtain Capabilities]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Application Layer Protocol]:::tactic
    C -->|T1568| D[Dynamic Resolution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1583.005| E[Botnet]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-36919: Sourcecodester Online Reviewer System v1.0 is vulnerale to SQL Injection in the ...
items.description: Sourcecodester Online Reviewer System v1.0 is vulnerale to SQL Injection in the file /system/system/admins/assessments/examproper/exam-update.php.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-36919 is a SQL Injection vulnerability in Sourcecodester Online Reviewer System v1.0, specifically in the /system/system/admins/assessments/examproper/exam-update.php file. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by sending a malicious request to the affected file, allowing them to inject and execute arbitrary SQL code. This can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, modification of exam results, and potential data breaches. To remediate this vulnerability, update to Sourcecodester Online Reviewer System v1.1 or later, which includes a patch for this issue. Additionally, ensure that the exam-update.php file is properly sanitized and validated to prevent similar attacks in the future.

items.description: Sourcecodester Basic Library System v1.0 is vulnerable to SQL Injection in /librarysystem/load_student.php.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-36874 is a SQL Injection vulnerability in Sourcecodester Basic Library System v1.0, specifically in the /librarysystem/load_student.php file. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by sending a malicious HTTP request, allowing them to inject arbitrary SQL code and potentially extract sensitive information. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to student data, which could be used for identity theft or other malicious purposes. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to Sourcecodester Basic Library System v1.1 or later, which includes a patch for this issue. Additionally, users should ensure that their web application firewall (WAF) is configured to block SQL Injection attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-34476: Server-Side Request Forgery via SW-URL Header vulnerability in Apache SkyWalking...
items.description:
Server-Side Request Forgery via SW-URL Header vulnerability in Apache SkyWalking MCP.

This issue affects Apache SkyWalking MCP: 0.1.0.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 0.2.0, which fixes this issue.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-34476 is a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in Apache SkyWalking MCP version 0.1.0, allowing attackers to exploit public-facing applications via the SW-URL header. If exploited, this vulnerability enables threat actors to bypass security controls and access internal systems, potentially leading to data breaches or privilege escalation. To remediate this issue, users must upgrade to Apache SkyWalking MCP version 0.2.0, which addresses this vulnerability. This upgrade is crucial to prevent SSRF attacks and protect against potential real-world impacts. Specifically, users should follow the upgrade instructions provided by the Apache SkyWalking project to ensure a seamless transition.

items.title: CVE-2025-15632: A vulnerability has been found in 1Panel-dev MaxKB up to 2.4.2. Impacted is an u...
items.description: A vulnerability has been found in 1Panel-dev MaxKB up to 2.4.2. Impacted is an unknown function of the file ui/src/chat.ts of the component MdPreview. items.title: AI Can Autonomously Hack Cloud Systems With Minimal Oversight: Researchers
items.description: Palo Alto Networks has developed Zealot, a multi-agent penetration testing PoC capable of reconnaissance, exploitation, and exfiltration. The post AI Can Autonomously Hack Cloud Systems With Minimal Oversight: Researchers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: 'Zealot' Shows What AI's Capable of in Staged Cloud Attack
items.description: The proof of concept revealed AI-based attacks unfold too fast for human defenders to respond, and that AI evinced more autonomous behavior than expected.

items.title: China-Linked GopherWhisper Infects 12 Mongolian Government Systems with Go Backdoors
items.description: Mongolian governmental institutions have emerged as the target of a previously undocumented China-aligned advanced persistent threat (APT) group tracked as GopherWhisper. "The group wields a wide array of tools mostly written in Go, using injectors and loaders to deploy and execute various backdoors in its arsenal," Slovakian cybersecurity company ESET said in a report shared with The Hacker

items.title: Apple Patches iOS Flaw Allowing Recovery of Deleted Chats
items.description: Apple rolled out the security patches for dozens of iPhone and iPad models and generations. The post Apple Patches iOS Flaw Allowing Recovery of Deleted Chats appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Vercel Finds More Compromised Accounts in Context.ai-Linked Breach
items.description: Vercel on Wednesday revealed that it has identified an additional set of customer accounts that were compromised as part of a security incident that enabled unauthorized access to its internal systems. The company said it made the discovery after expanding its investigation to include an extra set of compromise indicators, alongside a review of requests to the Vercel network and environment

items.title: Apple Patches iOS Flaw That Stored Deleted Signal Notifications in FBI Forensic Case
items.description: Apple has rolled out a software fix for iOS and iPadOS to address a Notification Services flaw that stored notifications marked for deletion on the device. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-28950 (CVSS score: N/A), has been described as a logging issue that has been addressed with improved data redaction. "Notifications marked for deletion could be unexpectedly retained on the device,"

items.description: Not nearly as polite as the name suggests, the ransomware gang has impressed researchers with its speed in scaling up operations — and its sophistication.

items.description: Researchers said the group stole up to $12 million in cryptocurrency in the first three months of 2026 through malware attacks on personal devices.

items.description: Hackers deployed a previously unknown wiper malware against Venezuela’s energy and utilities sector in an attack that appears to have been designed to destroy systems.

items.title: Kyber ransomware gang toys with post-quantum encryption on Windows
items.description: A new Kyber ransomware operation is targeting Windows systems and VMware ESXi endpoints in recent attacks, with one variant implementing Kyber1024 post-quantum encryption. [...]

items.title: Malicious KICS Docker Images and VS Code Extensions Hit Checkmarx Supply Chain
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of malicious images pushed to the official "checkmarx/kics" Docker Hub repository. In an alert published today, software supply chain security company Socket revealed that unknown threat actors managed to have overwritten existing tags, including v2.1.20 and alpine, while also introducing a new v2.1.21 tag that does not correspond to an official release. The

items.title: Self-Propagating Supply Chain Worm Hijacks npm Packages to Steal Developer Tokens
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a fresh set of packages that have been compromised by bad actors to deliver a self-propagating worm that spreads through stolen developer npm tokens. The supply chain worm has been detected by both Socket and StepSecurity, with the companies tracking the activity under the name CanisterSprawl owing to the use of an ICP canister to exfiltrate the stolen data

items.title: French police arrest suspected hacker behind dozens of data breaches
items.description: French authorities have arrested a suspected hacker believed to be behind dozens of data breaches targeting public institutions, sports federations and private organizations across the country.

items.title: Harvester Deploys Linux GoGra Backdoor in South Asia Using Microsoft Graph API
items.description: The threat actor known as Harvester has been attributed to a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor deployed as part of attacks likely targeting entities in South Asia. "The malware uses the legitimate Microsoft Graph API and Outlook mailboxes as a covert command-and-control (C2) channel, allowing it to bypass traditional perimeter network defenses," the Symantec and Carbon Black Threat Hunter

items.title: Spain dismantles major $4.7M manga piracy platform, arrests four
items.description: The Spanish police have dismantled the largest Spanish-language manga piracy platform, operating since 2014, with millions of monthly users from around the globe. [...]

items.title: DPRK Fake Job Scams Self-Propagate in 'Contagious Interview'
items.description: A compromised developer's repository serves as a worm-like infection vector to spread remote access Trojans (RATs) and other malware.

items.title: After Bluesky, Mastodon Targeted in DDoS Attack
items.description: The DDoS attack caused a major outage, but Mastodon mitigated it within a few hours. The post After Bluesky, Mastodon Targeted in DDoS Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Inside Caller-as-a-Service Fraud: The Scam Economy Has a Hiring Process
items.description: Fraud operations now operate like call centers, complete with hiring, training, and performance tracking. Flare reveals how cybercriminals manage "Caller-as-a-Service" operations like a professional sales team. [...]

items.title: China’s cyber capabilities now equal to the US, warns Dutch intelligence
items.description: Dutch intelligence says the threat from Beijing is now largely going unmet and is so sophisticated its operations are regularly missed by intelligence agencies and cybersecurity defenders.

items.title: New npm supply-chain attack self-spreads to steal auth tokens
items.description: A new supply chain attack targeting the Node Package Manager (npm) ecosystem is stealing developer credentials and attempting to spread through packages published from compromised accounts. [...]

items.title: Most Serious Cyberattacks Against the UK Now From Russia, Iran and China, Cyber Chief Says
items.description: British businesses need to prepare themselves to defend against cyberattacks because the U.K. could be targeted “at scale,” if it became involved in an international conflict. The post Most Serious Cyberattacks Against the UK Now From Russia, Iran and China, Cyber Chief Says appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: UK cyber agency handling four major incidents a week as nation-state attacks surge
items.description: Britain's cybersecurity chief warned Tuesday that the country is handling four nationally significant cyber incidents every week, with the majority now traced back to hostile foreign governments rather than criminal hackers.

items.title: Microsoft Teams to get efficiency mode on PCs with limited resources
items.description: Microsoft is preparing to roll out a new Efficiency Mode for Microsoft Teams for systems with limited CPU and memory resources to improve app responsiveness. [...]

items.title: New Wiper Malware Targeted Venezuelan Energy Sector Prior to US Intervention
items.description: Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the malware targets recovery mechanisms, overwrites drives, and systematically deletes files. The post New Wiper Malware Targeted Venezuelan Energy Sector Prior to US Intervention appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Mirai Botnet Targets Flaw in Discontinued D-Link Routers
items.description: The exploitation of the command injection vulnerability started one year after public disclosure and PoC exploit code publication. The post Mirai Botnet Targets Flaw in Discontinued D-Link Routers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Are SBOMs Failing? Supply Chain Attacks Rise as Security Teams Struggle With SBOM Data
items.description: Researcher says the missing piece is a governance-driven intelligence layer that turns SBOM and VEX data into explainable security decisions. The post Are SBOMs Failing? Supply Chain Attacks Rise as Security Teams Struggle With SBOM Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Claude Mythos Finds 271 Firefox Vulnerabilities
items.description: All the flaws could have also been found by an elite human researcher, according to Mozilla. The post Claude Mythos Finds 271 Firefox Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Lotus Wiper Malware Targets Venezuelan Energy Systems in Destructive Attack
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented data wiper that has been used in attacks targeting Venezuela at the end of last year and the start of 2026. Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the novel file wiper has been used in a destructive campaign targeting the energy and utilities sector in Venezuela, per findings from Kaspersky. "Two batch scripts are responsible for initiating the

items.title: North Korean Hackers Use AppleScript, ClickFix in Fresh macOS Attacks
items.description: The campaigns focus on financial organizations, including cryptocurrency, venture capital, and blockchain entities. The post North Korean Hackers Use AppleScript, ClickFix in Fresh macOS Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
items.description: On January 31, 2026, researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network built for AI agents, had left its database wide open, exposing 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active agents. The more worrying part sat inside the private messages. Some of those conversations held plaintext third-party credentials, including OpenAI API keys shared between agents,

items.title: Microsoft traces Universal Print issues to Graph API code change
items.description: Microsoft says that an ongoing Universal Print sharing issue that prevents users from creating some printer shares is due to a Microsoft Graph API code change. [...]

items.title: New GoGra malware for Linux uses Microsoft Graph API for comms
items.description: A Linux variant of the GoGra backdoor uses legitimate Microsoft infrastructure, relying on an Outlook inbox for stealthy payload delivery. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Patches Critical ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 Privilege Escalation Bug
items.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-40372, carries a CVSS score of 9.1 out of 10.0. It's rated Important in severity. An anonymous researcher has been credited with discovering and reporting the flaw. "Improper verification of cryptographic

items.title: Microsoft releases emergency patches for critical ASP.NET flaw
items.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band (OOB) security updates to patch a critical ASP.NET Core privilege escalation vulnerability. [...]

items.title: Mustang Panda’s New LOTUSLITE Variant Targets India Banks, South Korea Policy Circles
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new variant of a known malware called LOTUSLITE that's distributed via a theme related to India's banking sector. "The backdoor communicates with a dynamic DNS-based command-and-control server over HTTPS and supports remote shell access, file operations, and session management, indicating a continued espionage-focused capability set rather than

items.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to BlackCat Scheme
items.description: A cautionary tale illustrates why the person negotiating should never be involved with any part of the ransom payment process.

items.title: Exploits Turn Windows Defender Into Attacker Tool
items.description: Three proof-of-concept exploits are being used in active attacks against Microsoft's built-in security platform; two are unpatched.

items.title: Surge in Bomgar RMM Exploitation Demonstrates Supply Chain Risk
items.description: The critical remote code execution flaw (CVE-2026-1731) in the remote monitoring and management tool can be exploited to spread ransomware and compromise supply chains.

items.title: Google Fixes Critical RCE Flaw in AI-Based 'Antigravity' Tool
items.description: The prompt-injection vulnerability in the agentic AI product for filesystem operations was a sanitization issue that allowed for sandbox escape and arbitrary code execution.

items.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Chinese APT Targets Indian Banks, Korean Policy Circles
items.description: China is spying on India's financial sector, for some reason, and it's not putting much effort into it, judging by some stale TTPs.

items.title: A .WAV With A Payload, (Tue, Apr 21st)
items.description: There have been reports of threat actors using a .wav file as a vector for malware.

items.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
items.description: Stolen OAuth tokens, which are at the root of these breaches, "are the new attack surface, the new lateral movement," a researcher notes.

items.title: Serial-to-IP Devices Hide Thousands of Old & New Bugs
items.description: The OT devices that translate machine talk into Internet-speak are riddled with vulnerabilities and more frequently targeted for attacks, researchers say.

items.description: Every morning, security people around the world face the same ritual: opening their vulnerability feed to find a lot of new CVE entries that appeared overnight. Over the past decade, this flood has become a defining challenge of modern defensive security. Some numbers[1]:

items.description: Introduction

items.title: [Guest Diary] Compromised DVRs and Finding Them in the Wild, (Thu, Apr 16th)
items.description: &#x5b;This is a Guest Diary by Alec Jaffe, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&&#x23&#x3b;39&#x3b;s Degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program &#x5b;1].

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
items.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines what is knowable about Dort based on public information.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure

CISA Urges Critical Infrastructure Organizations to Take Action Against Insider Threats
CISA Releases Product Categories List to Propel Post-Quantum Cryptography Adoption Pursuant to President Trump’s Executive Order 14306
CISA, UK NCSC, FBI Unveil Principles to Combat Cyber Risks in OT
CISA Retires Ten Emergency Directives, Marking an Era in Federal Cybersecurity
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
